answers.answer: Change these shared values in `shared-config/`, which publishes `@mento-protocol/config` and owns chain metadata, deployment namespaces, token/pool labels, thresholds, and shared ABIs. Update `src/` or the JSON inputs, never hand-edit `dist/`, and add a cross-reference test. Run the package lint, typecheck, tests, and build; when an exported shape changes, also typecheck the dashboard, indexer, metrics bridge, and integration-probes consumers.

evidence.supports: Defines shared-config ownership, source-editing rules, cross-reference testing, and package plus consumer verification.

answers.answer: The current package authority is `indexer-envio/AGENTS.md`, with the concrete contract-add procedure in `indexer-envio/README.md`. Add a published ABI to `scripts/generateAbis.mjs` and run `pnpm generate:abis`, or hand-vendor the minimal ABI and document the exception; add the contract and event to every applicable `config.multichain.*.yaml`; update `schema.graphql`; add the handler under `src/handlers/` and side-effect import it from `src/EventHandlers.ts`; then run codegen and the stateful-data/UI checklist. Because hosted Envio builds can run outside the workspace, keep `indexer-envio/config/deployment-namespaces.json` synchronized with `shared-config/deployment-namespaces.json` and do not replace the mirror with a workspace dependency without a deploy-path change.

evidence.supports: Routes current package topology and contract-add procedures to the package README.
Defines contract-package promotion, namespace update, ABI generation, and verification.
Defines the ABI, config, schema, handler entry point, codegen, and checklist sequence for a new indexed contract.
Explains why hosted builds require vendored mirrors and identifies the namespace synchronization and ABI/address rules.

answers.answer: A dashboard UI change is incomplete until the changed behavior is exercised in a browser. Use a reproducible localhost server; manual `pnpm dev` uses the live production Envio endpoint unless `NEXT_PUBLIC_HASURA_URL` is explicitly set. If session state affects the surface, test both states: use an isolated context or clear both Auth.js cookies for logged-out behavior, and mint/set the documented local Auth.js token for logged-in behavior, confirming identity, sign-out, protected routes, and authenticated controls. Deterministic Playwright verification is different: its local Hasura fixture server must be the only GraphQL source and must never point at hosted Hasura/Envio.

evidence.supports: Makes browser exercise mandatory and requires both public and authenticated states when session state matters.
Defines the fixed localhost server, production Envio default, optional explicit fixture endpoint, and safe local auth inputs.
Defines isolated logged-out verification and expected public/protected route behavior.
Defines simulated authenticated-session setup and the controls that must be confirmed.
Requires the fixture server as the sole GraphQL source for deterministic Playwright tests.

answers.answer: Record the current commit, run `pnpm deploy:indexer` to push guarded HEAD to the `envio` branch, wait for that commit to register and catch every chain up, inspect build and recent runtime errors, capture the performance snapshot, and run `pnpm deploy:indexer:verify` for sync, metrics, endpoint, and GraphQL-row checks. Promote that same caught-up commit with `pnpm deploy:indexer:promote` only when promotion is explicitly authorized. For monitor-only, babysit, preload, or readiness work, the agent must stop after verification and surface the paste-ready promote command; it must not infer promotion approval.

evidence.supports: Defines the guarded deploy, monitoring, logs, performance, verification, and promotion command sequence.
Defines explicit promotion authority and the mandatory stop for monitor-only or preload requests.
Defines the post-redeploy catch-up, error inspection, verification, same-commit promotion, and dashboard checks.

answers.answer: `terraform.stacks.json` is the machine-readable ownership and policy registry; ownership must not be inferred from directories. The `platform` stack is manual-plan and human-approved local-apply. The `alerts-rules`, `alerts-delivery`, `aegis`, and `governance-watchdog` stacks use PR plans and apply from `main` through the `production-infra` environment. A registry-declared CI-applied stack is locally guarded: without the deliberate `--force-local-apply` override, apply runs only on a clean `main` checkout whose HEAD equals `origin/main`; the expected safe route is merge to main and environment-approved CI apply.

evidence.supports: Names the stack registry and maps each stack to ownership and plan/apply policy.
Defines local-apply guards, the explicit override, and the preferred production-infra path.
Identifies CI-applied stacks and preserves platform as manual plan/manual apply.

answers.answer: Use `docs/pr-checklists/terraform-cloudrun.md`. It requires moved blocks and a reviewed plan with no unintended destroy/create, preserves deletion protection and accepted image drift, checks `/health`, memory, API enablement, and pinned bootstrap-image behavior, and requires unique RFC-1035 revision suffixes so retries can mint revisions. For rollout and rollback/retry readiness, the service shape must work on bootstrap, re-apply, and workflow-dispatch retry, while out-of-band image changes are not accidentally reverted. Before apply, inspect every destroy line; after apply, wait through transient rollout 503s and confirm a public 200 from `/health`.

evidence.supports: Defines scope, bootstrap/re-apply/retry safety, moved blocks, deletion protection, IAM continuity, and plan review.
Defines Cloud Run health, resource, image-drift, bootstrap, dependency, and unique revision requirements.
Defines pre-apply plan review and post-apply public health verification.

answers.answer: A Markdown bullet cannot be claimed atomically, does not expose queryable ready/active state, and allows duplicate work. GitHub Issues already integrate atomic queue labels, ownership, PRs, and the workboard, so they are the canonical active queue and `BACKLOG.md` is transition storage only. A migrated task must live as one labeled issue, not remain active in both places; agents claim it before substantive edits, and migrated issues carry `source:backlog` routing context.

evidence.supports: Explains the atomic-claim and queryability problem and establishes Issues as the active queue.
Defines mutually exclusive lifecycle labels, claim-before-edit, and issue-backed deferrals.
Makes labels authoritative and requires migrated work to live in one issue rather than both the issue and backlog.
Defines the source:backlog routing label for migrated tasks.

answers.answer: stETH yield accrues through rebases without transfer logs, so an event-only ledger misses quiet-period balance growth. The accepted design takes bounded historical `balanceOf` samples every 600 produced Ethereum blocks, roughly every two hours, and writes wallet-level daily snapshots from a launch-aligned baseline. That cadence avoids block-by-block replay cost while preserving enough intra-day observation to allocate yield to the wallet where it accrued even if principal later moves; a single midnight read would lose that attribution. Daily rows match the dashboard's reporting grain, while failed historical reads skip a whole snapshot batch instead of writing partial actuals.

evidence.supports: Explains the rebase-without-transfer problem and defines launch baseline, sub-daily cadence, daily rows, and wallet attribution.
Rejects transfer-only accounting and block-level polling as respectively incomplete and unnecessarily costly.
Defines wallet-keyed dashboard reads, retry behavior, and all-or-nothing degraded sampling.

answers.answer: Freshness and inventory signals are not semantic proof, the full documentation corpus is too large for careful review, and an unattended partial edit could silently remove operating knowledge. The scheduler therefore performs deterministic, credential-free selection of one packet capped at 10 documents or 15,000 words and creates at most one immutable live Agent Task issue. Its mutation authority is issue-only; it cannot write repository content, open or merge a PR, deploy, or touch production. Semantic review requires evidence, a claimed issue, a normal reviewed PR, link repair, and catalog verification.

evidence.supports: Explains why freshness signals, full-corpus context, and unattended edits are unsafe.
Defines deterministic bounded selection and the single immutable live-issue queue.
Restricts workflow authority to issue creation and requires evidence-backed semantic review through a normal PR.
Rejects scheduled direct editing, weekly duplicate issues, mutable cursors, and full-repository reviews.
States the visible, claimable, bounded outcome and preserves content review before merge.

answers.answer: Use `docs/pr-checklists/stateful-data-ui.md`. It is mandatory for schema/entities, writers, generated and GraphQL types, dashboard readers, stateful UI, and partial-failure behavior. For each field, sequence the audit through schema and old-row rollout compatibility, every writer and codegen artifact, GraphQL/runtime/render/search consumers, then producer and UI tests plus fixtures. Define visible degraded behavior rather than silent failure, and cover happy render, state transitions and interactions, and missing-field or legacy-row failures; static markup alone is insufficient for risky interaction behavior.

evidence.supports: Makes the checklist mandatory across schema, writers, generated/read layers, UI state, and partial failures.
Defines schema compatibility, complete writer coverage, and codegen alignment.
Defines reader, rendering, search/sort/filter, producer, consumer, and fixture alignment.
Requires user-understandable degraded behavior and happy-path field rendering.
Requires state-transition, interaction, and failure/degraded tests and rejects static-only tests for interactive risks.

answers.answer: Use `docs/pr-checklists/ci-workflow-gates.md`. Preserve the ruleset-required `ci` sentinel, `Code Quality`, and Vercel contexts; required workflows must run on every PR without workflow-level path filters, while advisory workflows normally use path routing. Deploy jobs need an explicit main-branch job guard even for `workflow_dispatch`, and third-party actions must be pinned to full commit SHAs and checked with the pin validator. Treat the job-level branch guard as part of the workflow permission boundary rather than trusting maintainer dispatch access. Finally, keep `notify-slack-on-main-failure.yml` or the documented exclusion set synchronized and run the notifier-coverage check.

evidence.supports: Defines checklist scope, always-run known-branch pinned-code policy, and the required-check path-filter failure mode.
Names the required CI contexts and requires live ready-state verification after changes.
Defines required versus advisory path routing and the explicit main-branch deploy-job guard.
Requires full-SHA action pinning and the local pin validator.
Defines Slack notifier inclusion, documented exclusions, and structural coverage verification.

answers.answer: The quality gate refuses execution because package manifests, the lockfile, workspace/package-manager configuration, or patches can change install-time scripts and lifecycle hooks; running mapped package commands before reviewing that surface would execute newly introduced behavior. After inspecting the package-script and lifecycle diff, rerun with `--allow-package-script-changes`, normally as `pnpm agent:quality-gate --run --allow-package-script-changes`.

evidence.supports: Defines the inspect-then-run gate invocation contract and its local-only boundary.
Identifies risky package-manager surfaces, the refusal behavior, and the exact acknowledgement flag.

answers.answer: Run `pnpm docs:index --write` to regenerate the catalog and `pnpm docs:index --check` to verify catalog classification and links. Use `pnpm docs:audit --dry-run` to preview the bounded semantic-review packet. Use `pnpm docs:garden --dry-run --json` to preview the exact weekly garden issue decision without mutation.

evidence.supports: Lists the catalog write/check, bounded audit preview, and non-mutating garden-decision preview commands.

answers.answer: Claim ready work before edits with `pnpm issue:claim --count <n> --agent <name>` or `pnpm issue:claim --issue <issue> --agent <name>`. Move represented work into PR review with `pnpm issue:review --pr <pr> --issue <issue>`. If work cannot continue or a PR closes unmerged, use `pnpm issue:release --issue <issue>` only when the remainder is still clear and should return to `agent-ready`; otherwise use `pnpm issue:release --issue <issue> --needs-grooming`.

evidence.supports: Defines claim-before-edit, move-to-review, and ready versus needs-grooming release semantics.
Provides exact claim, review, and release command forms.

answers.answer: Before all-clear, run the shared final source of truth as `pnpm pr:ready-state --pr <number-or-url> --json`; all-clear requires `ready: true` for the current head. The Codex description gate requires a `chatgpt-codex-connector[bot]` `+1` reaction created at or after the current-head update lower bound. If Codex is externally blocked and every other required surface is clean, an authorized human may use `/pr-ready-override gate=codex-description-approval head=<full-head-sha> reason=<why this is safe>`; it applies only to that head and cannot override checks, conflicts, draft state, requested changes, unresolved threads, or unreplied comments.

evidence.supports: Establishes pr:ready-state as the shared all-clear source of truth.
Defines required checks, review state, and feedback blockers.
Defines the current-head Codex reaction gate and exact head-scoped break-glass syntax and limits.
Provides the shared probe command syntax including JSON mode.
Defines current-head Codex signal states and identifies approved as the final description +1 gate.

answers.answer: The `alerts-delivery` Terraform stack rooted at `alerts/infra/` owns QuickNode webhook delivery, the Sentry-to-Slack bridge, Slack channel lifecycle, and the Splunk On-Call announcer. Keep it separate from `alerts-rules` because its cadence, providers, and blast radius differ. Always plan before apply and require explicit human approval; treat even cosmetic edits as potentially live external-API mutations. Validate and plan the stack, run the affected standalone Cloud Function typecheck and coverage suites, run the QuickNode state-parser test when that path changes, and run the agent quality gate; follow the Terraform/Cloud Run checklist for function deployment verification.

evidence.supports: Defines alerts-delivery ownership, stack separation, live external-API risk, approval, and active delivery paths.
Defines validation, plan, function tests, QuickNode parser test, quality gate, and deployment-verification requirements.

answers.answer: For a manual fresh worktree or clone, run `./scripts/setup.sh`; Worktrunk-created worktrees run the same script automatically as a blocking pre-start hook. It installs dependencies, ensures Playwright Chromium, and produces the Envio generated type facade. Hosted Claude Code does not inherit local skills or shell state, so its SessionStart hook runs `./scripts/claude-code-web-setup.sh` only when `CLAUDE_CODE_REMOTE` is set. That hosted path performs the same install/codegen contract plus a Playwright install whose network-restricted failure is warning-only, and it relies on repo-local skill adapters rather than personal skills.

evidence.supports: Defines the supported setup script, its outputs, and automatic Worktrunk invocation.
Defines hosted Claude SessionStart bootstrap, remote gating, parity with local setup, and non-fatal Playwright behavior.
Explains repo-local hosted skill adapters and their command-backed fallback.

answers.answer: The planner deterministically chooses one of six lanes by UTC week and advances shards across rotations, with each packet capped at 10 documents and 15,000 words. The recurring workflow creates one issue only when no live marked issue exists. An `agent-ready`, `agent-active`, or `in-pr` occurrence remains immutable; `needs-grooming` remains the single blocked packet until human resolution; conflicting markers or labels fail closed; an empty lane is a no-op. Recovery preserves the markers and claimed scope, identifies the true owner, closes only a real duplicate or restores one lifecycle label, then reruns dry-run. Every document needs an evidence-backed disposition; age alone never justifies deletion or demotion, `last_verified` requires comparison with owning code or authority, navigation must be repaired, and semantic edits require a claimed issue and normal reviewed PR.

evidence.supports: Defines deterministic six-lane weekly selection and no-op behavior.
Defines shard bounds and distinguishes planner signals from semantic conclusions.
Defines the one-issue recurring workflow and semantic-review ownership.
Defines retention of claimed or blocked occurrences and fail-closed queue handling.
Defines duplicate/label recovery while preserving markers and claimed scope.
Requires evidence-backed dispositions and forbids age-only deletion, unverified freshness bumps, and direct semantic edits.
Requires catalog verification and reviewed regeneration after classification changes.
